name: factory_boy
what_it_does: Test factory library for Python — creates test data using declarative factory definitions. factory_boy features: Factory class declaration (DjangoModelFactory, SQLAlchemyModelFactory), factory.build() for in-memory, factory.create() for DB-persisted, factory.Sequence for unique values, factory.Faker for realistic fake data, factory.SubFactory for associations, factory.LazyAttribute for computed fields, factory.Trait for variations, batch creation (factory.build_batch), @factory.post_generation hooks, and create_batch for N records. Django equivalent of Ruby's FactoryBot. Integrates with Faker for realistic data.
use_cases: Agent Django test data — AgentFactory.create(name='SearchAgent', status='idle') creates Agent model instance with defaults; factory defined with class AgentFactory(DjangoModelFactory): class Meta: model = Agent; agent test setup in 1 line vs 10 lines of manual model creation

Agent with associations — TaskFactory.create(agent=AgentFactory.create()) creates Task with associated Agent; factory.SubFactory(AgentFactory) in TaskFactory auto-creates agent if not provided; agent relationship tests use factory composition without manual FK management

Unique value sequences — factory.Sequence(lambda n: f'agent-{n}@example.com') generates unique emails per factory call; agent tests creating multiple users avoid unique constraint violations; sequences are thread-safe and reset with factory.reset_sequence()

Realistic agent test data — name = factory.Faker('name'); email = factory.Faker('email'); ip = factory.Faker('ipv4'); agent factories produce human-realistic test data using Faker library; tests are more representative than 'test_name_1' placeholder strings

Agent factory traits — class AgentFactory(DjangoModelFactory): class Params: active = factory.Trait(status='active', last_ping=timezone.now()); AgentFactory.create(active=True) creates active agent; test scenarios defined as traits not repetitive attribute overrides
not_for: Non-Django/SQLAlchemy ORMs — factory_boy has built-in support for Django and SQLAlchemy; for other ORMs use base Factory with custom _create classmethod
Production data seeding — factory_boy is for tests; for database seeds use Django data migrations or management commands
Complex state machine factories — factory_boy doesn't model state transitions; for complex lifecycle testing, build each state factory separately
best_when: Your Django or SQLAlchemy agent app needs clean, composable test data factories — factory_boy is the Python standard for creating test agent records with realistic fake data, associations, and trait variations.
avoid_when: You prefer plain pytest fixtures with direct ORM calls, your ORM isn't supported, or you need production seeding.

auth.notes: No auth — local test library that interacts with Django ORM or SQLAlchemy directly.

pricing.notes: factory_boy is MIT licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — test data generation. create_batch(100, :agent) creates 100 DB records. Each create() is a separate ORM save() call — use bulk_create for very large fixtures.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Django validation errors from factory creation surface as ValidationError. IntegrityError on unique constraint violations from sequence misconfiguration. AttributeError if factory field references non-existent model field. SubFactory circular dependency raises RecursionError.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: factory.build() is idempotent for same params (no sequences). Sequences increment per-call — not idempotent. factory.create() DB records persist per ORM transaction. Django TestCase rolls back between tests — factory data doesn't persist.
known_agent_gotchas: DjangoModelFactory requires Django setup — import factory; class AgentFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory) fails with django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured if Django settings not configured; agent test files must import django.conf.settings or run via pytest-django with DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE set

factory.build() vs factory.create() confusion — build() returns unsaved model instance (no DB); create() saves to DB; agent tests that call build() and then query the DB for the created object get zero results; use create() for DB-backed agent test data, build() for unit tests not touching DB

SubFactory creates associations on create not build — factory.SubFactory(AgentFactory) in TaskFactory calls AgentFactory.create() when TaskFactory.create() is called; this inserts an Agent even if test only needs Task; use factory.SubFactory(AgentFactory, _create=False) or build() to avoid unwanted DB inserts in agent unit tests

Sequence numbers are global per factory — factory.Sequence(lambda n: f'agent-{n}') increments globally across all test runs in a session; agent tests depending on specific sequence numbers (agent-0, agent-1) are fragile; design factories to assert uniqueness not specific values

factory.Faker locale must match test expectations — factory.Faker('name') uses default locale (en_US); agent tests expecting French names must use factory.Faker('name', locale='fr_FR'); mismatched locale causes name format assertion failures in agent internationalization tests

@factory.post_generation hooks receive extracted kwargs — @factory.post_generation def tags(self, create, extracted, **kwargs): if extracted: self.tags.set(extracted); AgentFactory.create(tags=[tag1, tag2]) passes list as extracted; agent factories with M2M relationships must use post_generation; forgetting create check causes M2M errors on build()

security_score_components.security_notes: Test-only library — no network access. Factory files may define test passwords or tokens — use non-realistic placeholders (never real credentials). Ensure factory files in tests/ directory not compiled into production package.
